telltalePatterns: voiced interdental fricative /ð/ substitution with alveolar stop /d/

prosody.intonation: L1-influenced (flatter pitch contour, less dynamic range)
prosody.fluencyNotes: Generally fluent with appropriate pausing. Connected speech lacks some native-like reductions and linking.

strengths: Clear production of most consonant sounds (e.g., /s/, /m/, /tʃ/)
Generally accurate and distinct vowel sounds

recommendedFocus.reason: Consistent substitution with /d/ significantly impacts intelligibility and marks speech as non-native. Mastering this will make a noticeable difference.
A flatter intonation can make speech sound less natural and engaging. Developing more dynamic pitch patterns and appropriate word stress will improve naturalness and comprehension.
Incorporating more natural reductions (e.g., of function words) and smoother linking between words will improve fluency and make speech sound more native-like.

result.transcript: It's funny how some people always seem to have their lives together while I'm just trying to find my matching socks.
